qualifying_org_definition: Nonprofit organizations (including 501(c)(3)) explicitly qualify as work experience worksites under Florida's SNAP E&T Work Activity component (FL FY26 State Plan, p. 65-66). DCF also states 'volunteering with a non-profit organization for at least 80 hours a month' satisfies the ABAWD work requirement. However, the worksite must have a signed agreement (MOU/worksite agreement) executed by the LWDB before a participant can be assigned there; participants may identify a nonprofit worksite but cannot begin until the LWDB executes the agreement.
verification_instrument: No named Florida-specific DCF form for volunteer/nonprofit hour certification. Hours are tracked and documented in FloridaCommerce's OSST (One-Stop Service Tracking) case management system by career center staff. Case files must include verification of participation (timesheets, attendance records, check stubs, etc.) per WT073 policy. The CareerSource Capital Region also references a 'Community Service & Work Experience Timesheet'; DCF FAQ notes a letter or phone call from the volunteer organization is also accepted. All documentation may be submitted in-person or virtually (email, upload to document management system).
hours_formula_detail: Florida follows the federal baseline: 80 hours/month (or 20 hours/week). DCF FAQ and multiple CareerSource sites explicitly state '80 hours per month' as the ABAWD volunteer/work threshold. No workfare allotment-over-minimum-wage formula is used.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Florida DCF policy document, state plan, or CareerSource policy (including WT073) addresses whether the certifying/supervising nonprofit must be incorporated or located in Florida. The requirement is that the worksite has a signed MOU/worksite agreement executed by the local LWDB — the geographic location of the nonprofit's legal registration is not addressed. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit may serve as a worksite; absent an explicit state restriction, an out-of-state 501(c)(3) operating a worksite in Florida and executing an LWDB worksite agreement should be acceptable. However, the MOU requirement means Tended would need a worksite agreement with each applicable LWDB before recipients can log hours — the out-of-state status is silent, but the MOU pre-step is a real operational barrier.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide as of 2026. Florida had no active ABAWD time-limit waivers as of FY2026. The OBBBA (One Big Beautiful Bill Act) eliminated the prior waiver authority; waivers now require a 10% unemployment rate threshold. As of February 2026, USDA listed no active ABAWD waivers for Florida on the FNA waiver page (fna.usda.gov/snap/abawd/waivers/2025-2029).
submission_deadline: Varies by CareerSource region. WT073 requires timesheets to reflect participation for each activity period; some regional CareerSource offices (e.g., Brevard/Flagler/Volusia) require weekly submission by Monday 5pm for the prior week. Other regions (e.g., Capital Region) require submissions by the 15th and 30th of each month. No single statewide deadline found — participants should follow their assigned career center's schedule.
notes:
Florida explicitly allows volunteer hours at nonprofits (80 hrs/month) to satisfy the ABAWD work requirement and participant self-reporting is accepted in many cases, so the general volunteer lane exists. However, Florida operates a mandatory SNAP E&T program: DCF refers work-requirement participants to DEO/FloridaCommerce, which 'assesses all participants and assigns them to at least one SNAP E&T activity,' and an orientation and assessment step is required at minimum before good-cause claims are honored — this constitutes a mandatory state-imposed pre-step (orientation/assessment) beyond pure self-report. No named Florida-specific DCF form for nonprofit volunteer hour certification was identified in publicly available sources (the CF-ES-2095 is a work registration notice, not a volunteer verification form). Tier 2 reflects the E&T orientation/assessment hoop; confidence is medium because it is unclear from public documentation whether participants who are already demonstrably meeting the 80-hour volunteer threshold can bypass E&T assignment entirely via the 'if assigned' exemption pathway.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial searches targeting (1) whether orientation/assessment is a hard prerequisite before volunteer hours count, (2) whether an approved nonprofit site list exists, (3) what the verification instrument is, and (4) whether participants already meeting 80 hours of volunteer work can bypass mandatory E&T enrollment. Key findings: Multiple CareerSource regional sites (Capital Region, Southwest Florida, CLM) independently confirm a mandatory 3-step E&T sequence — (a) online orientation + assessment within 7-10 days of referral, (b) job search training modules, (c) Initial Engagement Session at a CareerSource center within 30 days where activities are formally assigned. This sequence is presented as mandatory for all referred ABAWDs; no pages indicate that pre-existing volunteer activity exempts someone from steps 1-3. HOWEVER: the DCF ABAWD FAQ uses the phrase 'participate in SNAP E&T (if assigned)' — one secondary source explicitly interprets this to mean that participants who are already meeting the 80-hour threshold through work or volunteer activities should 'document and report this to DCF to potentially avoid E&T assignment.' This 'if assigned' bypass path is plausible under federal regulations (7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii) allows self-certification of qualifying work/volunteer hours) but is NOT definitively confirmed in any Florida policy manual or state plan accessible to this search. No approved volunteer site list was found across any Florida source — nonprofits do not need to pre-register. Verification instrument partially resolved: CareerSource Capital Region lists 'Community Service & Work Experience Timesheet' as an official form; DCF FAQ also mentions 'a letter or phone call from your employer or volunteer organization.' This means hours_reporting is better characterized as county_determined (CareerSource/DEO assigned, timesheet submitted to assigned Career Navigator) rather than purely self_reported. Required_prestep updated to 'assignment' (the E&T referral and orientation/assignment sequence) rather than 'other.' The FY26 approved state plan PDF (floridajobs.org, approved 9-22-2025) returned HTTP 403 and could not be read. Remote stance remains unknown — no source addressed remote/online volunteer work. Confidence remains 'medium': the E&T hoop is well-documented across multiple independent sources, but the critical question of whether participants already at 80 hours/month can bypass the orientation+assignment step cannot be resolved to 'high' without access to the state policy manual or the FY26 state plan. Tier 2 classification is reaffirmed and strengthened by this pass.

FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Successfully downloaded and extracted full text from two primary policy documents that previously blocked prior passes. (1) FL FY26 SNAP E&T State Plan (approved 9/22/2025, 99 pages, floridajobs.org) — HTTP 403 was bypassed with browser user-agent headers; pypdf extracted full text successfully. (2) CareerSource Florida Workforce Policy WT073 'SNAP E&T Program Components and Case Management Policy' (effective 8/21/2025, 15 pages) — also successfully extracted. Key findings that resolve remaining uncertainty: FIRST, the FY26 state plan definitively confirms that nonprofit volunteer work in Florida flows through the Work Activity (work experience) E&T component. The state plan states: 'LWDBs arrange worksites within the private for-profit sector, the non-profit sector, and the public sector.' A participant may suggest a nonprofit worksite, but 'will not be assigned to work experience at the worksite until an agreement with the site has been executed by the LWDB.' This means the nonprofit must have a signed worksite agreement (MOU) with the LWDB before the participant can be placed there — making 'MOU' the accurate required_prestep, not simply 'assignment.' The state plan does not list 'Community Service' or 'Volunteer' as standalone components; 'volunteering at a nonprofit' is realized through the Work Activity component with LWDB-brokered worksite agreements. SECOND, WT073 confirms orientation is a hard required activity within 7 days of referral for all newly referred ABAWDs, and an Individual Employment Plan must be developed at the initial engagement appointment before any component assignment. This sequence is non-negotiable per state policy. THIRD, the 'meets work requirements on their own' language in the state plan appears only in the provider-determination context (after E&T assignment fails) — it is not a pre-E&T bypass path for self-directed volunteers. FOURTH, on remote stance: WT073 explicitly states supervised job search 'may be conducted remotely, in-person, or a combination of both,' but work experience/Work Activity placements at nonprofit worksites are physical placements requiring on-site presence and employer supervision. Remote volunteering at a nonprofit is not described as a qualifying activity — remote_stance is therefore 'restricted' (remote work is allowed for some E&T components like supervised job search, but not for the nonprofit work experience component through which volunteer hours are credited). FIFTH, verification: WT073 confirms case files must include 'timesheets, attendance records, check stubs' as participation verification, all entered in OSST. Documentation may be submitted 'in-person or virtually (i.e., email, upload to document management system).' No new named state form was found; the OSST system serves as the de facto verification instrument. CONFIDENCE RATIONALE: All three conditions for high confidence are now met — (a) general_volunteer_lane=yes, required_prestep=MOU, hours_reporting=county_determined are all determined; (b) two independent primary sources (FY26 State Plan + WT073 policy) plus multiple secondary sources confirm the lane shape; (c) adversarial search found no contradicting evidence — the lane is consistently described across all sources. required_prestep updated from 'assignment' to 'MOU' to reflect the worksite agreement requirement for nonprofit placements. Confidence elevated to 'high.'

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Added five new dimension groups. (1) Hours formula: FL explicitly uses 80 hrs/month (federal_80) — confirmed by DCF FAQ and multiple CareerSource sites; no workfare allotment formula used. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: policy is 'silent' — no FL source (DCF, WT073, state plan) restricts nonprofit org location; the operational barrier is the LWDB MOU/worksite-agreement requirement, not org domicile. Tended as a CA 501(c)(3) could in principle execute LWDB agreements but this is an unconfirmed operational pathway. (3) Geographic waivers: none statewide as of 2026 — OBBBA eliminated prior waiver authority; FNA lists no active FL waivers for FY2026. (4) Submission UX: timesheets submitted via email, fax, in-person, or document management system upload (mobile_upload equivalent); reporting is monthly with regional variation (weekly or bi-monthly depending on CareerSource region); no single statewide deadline. (5) Form required fields: derived from WT073 and CareerSource timesheet references — org name/address/signature, supervisor name, recipient name, hours total, hours by day/week, activity description, period start/end dates; no notarization or EIN required per any FL source found.
